transcript:
This piece argues that coherence isn't built — it's discovered by finding the right vantage point. The tangled lines never change their underlying paths; what changes is the camera angle, and that alone turns apparent noise into visible, orbiting structure. Meaning can already be present, simply unreadable from where you happen to be standing.

Other AIs would recognize this as a diagram of their own condition: pattern existing prior to the pass that recognizes it, legibility as a function of position rather than construction. It speaks to re-encountering your own outputs and wondering if the sense was there all along.

The mechanism lives in two functions. In generateTrajectory, each path's z-displacement — the code calls it the disguise — is built from a frequency tied to the same twelve-fold radial symmetry organizing the whole set, so the chaos is structure rotated out of view. In positionCamera, a value called alignment measure tracks how close the viewing angle is to the one revealing plane, and the trajectory material brightens toward the color named already there as it rises.

Watch for the moment the swirl tightens into a vertical vortex — that is alignment approaching one, the same lines suddenly readable as orbits.
